core_concepts: CI/CD pipeline integration (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI)
supported_claims: Reduces manual review time by up to 50% based on Alibaba's internal usage data (source: project README)
Supports over 10 programming languages including Java, Python, Go, and JavaScript (source: official docs)
Integrates with GitHub Actions via a dedicated action in the marketplace (source: GitHub Actions marketplace)
Allows custom rule definitions using YAML configuration files (source: project wiki)
Provides both static analysis and LLM-based suggestions for code improvements (source: project blog)
common_pitfalls: Over-reliance on AI suggestions without human verification can introduce subtle bugs
Custom rule YAML syntax errors can silently disable rules
LLM-based review may produce inconsistent results across different models or API versions
Performance overhead when scanning large monorepos with many files
Misconfigured CI integration can cause duplicate reviews or missed triggers
research_questions: How does open-code-review compare to other AI code review tools like CodeRabbit or Amazon CodeGuru Reviewer?
What is the accuracy of LLM-based suggestions compared to rule-based checks in production environments?
How does the tool handle security-sensitive code (e.g., authentication logic) to avoid false positives?
What are the resource requirements (CPU, memory, API costs) for running the tool at scale?
Can the tool be extended to support custom domain-specific rules for proprietary frameworks?
success_criteria: Reduction in code review cycle time by at least 30% within the first month
Detection rate of at least 80% for common code quality issues (e.g., unused imports, null pointer risks)
Zero false positives for critical security rules (e.g., SQL injection patterns)
Successful integration with existing CI/CD pipeline without breaking builds
Developer satisfaction score of 4/5 or higher in post-implementation surveys
